Administration of how democracy works questions: CAPI RANDOMISATION: automated random allocation to Group 1 (C32-C34), Group 2 (C35-C37) or Group 3 (C38-C40), each comprising approximately one third of the sample. Administration of how important things are for democracy: [For question D28, we are including five different versions with the order of the response items rotated by one position each time. The filter variable for this (D28Order) is described below. Each respondent should be randomly assigned to one of the five versions. There are also five different showcards for these items to reflect the different orders.

Create random split variable D28Order which will NOT BE SHOWN to respondents. Each experimental group should include approximately 20% of respondents. This variable should be populated at the start of the interview.

It is also required to build in CAPI hard checks at D28CheckA/B/C/D/E. At each of these, interviewers must be asked to confirm that the correct showcard is being used before they are allowed to progress to the next question.]RECORD OUTCOME OF CAPI RANDOMISATION
